site.name: Sanata Münhasır
site.description: Traditional Ebru marbling art studio in Meram, Konya. In-person and online classes for students in Turkey and worldwide.

footer.address: Melikşah Mahallesi Yenice Sokak No:32 Meram, Konya, Turkey

hero.title:
A Journey into
Art on Water
hero.subtitle: A traditional Ebru studio in Meram, Konya — where colour meets water.

ebruIntro.label: Ebru Art
ebruIntro.quote: Ebru is not made — it reveals itself.
ebruIntro.body: Every pattern is the product of a unique meeting — pigments spreading freely on the surface of water. No two works of Ebru are ever alike, just as no two moments are ever the same.

studioIntro.title: A practice rooted in tradition
studioIntro.body: Atölye Sanata Münhasır was founded to preserve the living tradition of Turkish Ebru in Meram, Konya. This is not a course programme — it is a years-long practice built on discipline, patience, and stillness. Every session is built around focus, creation, and inner quiet.

courses.title: At your own pace, on your own path

items.desc: Materials, the preparation ritual, and first pattern experiments. Discover how a blank sheet of paper becomes a work of art in a water-filled tray.
Nightingale nest, Hatip, and advanced composition techniques. At this stage each design approaches the signature of a master.
We work with students from across Turkey and around the world. One-to-one video sessions with a materials guide so you can build your own Ebru corner at home.
Tailored studio experiences for school groups, corporate teams, and private occasions. Get in touch to arrange yours.

partA.label: Workshops
partA.title: Groups & Private Events
partA.body: We run bespoke Ebru workshops for school groups, adults, and corporate teams. Suitable for all ages and experience levels — discover Ebru together.

partB.label: Institutional Partnerships
partB.title: Schools & Organisations
partB.body: We build lasting partnerships with schools, cultural centres, and NGOs in Konya and beyond. Contact us to integrate Ebru into your curriculum or enrich your institutional events.

inPerson.label: In-Person
inPerson.title: Konya Studio
inPerson.body: Join us in the Melikşah Quarter for lessons in a traditional studio setting. A prepared kitre tray, specialist pigments, and a workspace refined under the guidance of a master. Every session is built around focus, creation, and stillness.

online.label: Online
online.title: Remote Studio
online.body: We work with students from across Turkey and around the world. With a materials list, preparation guide, and one-to-one video session support, you can build your own Ebru corner at home.

finalCta.title: Would you like to start learning Ebru today?
finalCta.body: Do you have a question? Would you like to begin a course? Write to us — we will get back to you promptly.

hero.title:
An Ebru Journey
from Tradition to Future
hero.subtitle: A living art practice from Meram, Konya, reaching the world.

instructor.name: Sema Baranok Yılmaz
instructor.bio: Born in Konya, Sema Baranok Yılmaz has been learning and teaching the subtleties of Ebru art for over 19 years. Preserving traditional techniques while developing contemporary interpretations, she has passed on this ancient art to hundreds of students. Her works have appeared in exhibitions at home and abroad, and she is recognised as a living practitioner of the Ebru tradition.

philosophy.title: Our Artistic Philosophy
philosophy.body: Our aim in teaching Ebru is not merely to pass on technique — it is to share the art of listening to the language of water, of waiting patiently, and of releasing the outcome. We open space for each student to find their own voice.

values.title: Fidelity to Tradition
values.desc: We teach centuries-old Ottoman Ebru techniques with faithful attention to their origins.

values.title: Individual Journey
values.desc: Each student advances at their own rhythm, in their own direction. There is no comparison.

values.title: Patience & Stillness
values.desc: Ebru is not for the hurried. Each session is a practice in meditation.

values.title: Living Tradition
values.desc: Bringing ancient wisdom into the modern world is the studio's founding purpose.

atelier.title: Together with Poyraz and Pera
atelier.body: Our studio in the Melikşah Quarter is a warm working space equipped with kitre trays, specialist Ebru pigments, poyraz brushes, and traditional materials. Our two beloved residents — Poyraz the parrot and Pera the fish — are the quiet witnesses of every lesson.

teaching.title: Language before technique
teaching.body: In our lessons, we first teach students to observe the behaviour of water. Understanding how pigments spread across the surface, feeling the correct brush pressure, listening to the rhythm of comb movement — these come before technique. Theory and practice are interwoven in every lesson.

steps.title: Materials and preparation
steps.desc: Meeting the kitre, pigments, and tray.

steps.title: The language of water
steps.desc: Observing surface tension and pigment behaviour.

steps.title: First patterns
steps.desc: First attempts with Battal and combed Ebru.

hero.title:
Every path to Ebru
begins here
hero.subtitle: From beginner to advanced, in-person to online — choose your own path.

items.title: 48-Hour Notice
items.desc: Each lesson is booked at least 48 hours in advance. This gives both student and teacher proper preparation time.

items.title: Monthly Planning Model
items.desc: Lessons run on a monthly scheduling system. Flexible planning lets you progress at your own pace.

items.title: Small Groups
items.desc: Classes are held in groups of maximum 4–6. Every student receives individual attention and personal feedback.

items.title: Pricing on Enquiry
items.desc: For pricing, please contact us via WhatsApp or the contact form. We will provide tailored information.

items.title: Beginner Studio
items.desc: Materials, the preparation ritual, and first pattern experiments. Introduction to the kitre tray, pigments, and brushes. Battal Ebru and simple combed patterns.

items.title: Advanced Studio
items.desc: Combed, shawl, and floral techniques. Nightingale nest and Hatip Ebru. Developing your own original compositions. Each design approaches a master's signature.

items.title: Online Private Lesson
items.desc: One-to-one video session regardless of location. Materials list and preparation guide included. Build your own Ebru corner at home.

items.title: Online Group Lesson
items.desc: Online sessions conducted with small groups. Community support, interactive learning, and the shared experience of creating together.

cta.title: Take the first step
cta.body: If you are unsure which class type suits you, ask us. Share your experience and goals and we will work out the best programme for you together.

hero.title: Traces from the studio
hero.subtitle: Every work shaped on water's surface is a singular moment.

hero.title: Get in Touch
hero.subtitle: For questions, class bookings, or collaboration enquiries.

hero.title: Thoughts on Ebru
hero.subtitle: Traces of art, history, and studio life.

posts.title: What Is Ebru Art?
posts.excerpt: An introduction to the roots and techniques of Ebru — the traditional Turkish art of marbling paper with pigment and comb on water.

posts.title: Learning Ebru in Konya
posts.excerpt: The preservation of traditional arts in the city of Mevlâna. The history and present-day significance of Ebru practice in Konya.

posts.title: Is Online Ebru Teaching Possible?
posts.excerpt: Teaching a tactile art in a digital environment — challenges, solutions, and the experience of remote Ebru learning.

posts.title: Where Do I Begin with Ebru?
posts.excerpt: Materials, expectations, and first steps. A comprehensive guide for those new to Ebru.
